avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet and gliding snow require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment: In all aspects medium-sized and large moist and wet avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming. On wind-loaded slopes the avalanches can penetrate even deep layers and reach quite a large size. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on steep sunny slopes above approximately 1700 m. In the regions exposed to a lot of new snow caution is to be exercised in particular at the base of rock walls and. In addition the fresh wind slabs in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain are capable of being triggered in some locations. At intermediate altitudes the danger is a little lower. Great caution and restraint are recommended. Backcountry tours should be started and concluded early.

snowpackStructure.comment: A lot of snow has fallen over a wide area. In addition sometimes large wind slabs formed in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls and generally in the high Alpine regions. New snow and wind slabs are lying mostly on a moist old snowpack. The spring-like weather conditions will give rise to rapid moistening of the snowpack on steep sunny slopes.

avalancheActivity.comment:
In some localities a partly overcast night. Further warming. The avalanche danger will already exist in the early morning. This applies in particular below approximately 2500 m.

As a consequence of warming and solar radiation, the natural activity of medium moist and wet avalanches will gradually increase, in particular on very steep sunny slopes. This also applies at the base of rock walls.

More recent wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in isolated cases in particular on shady slopes, this also applies at a distance from ridgelines, caution is to be exercised on very steep slopes at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The surface of the snowpack will soften earlier than the day before.
At higher altitudes, snow cover becomes thinner and patchy; it is better at mid-altitudes, which are less exposed to the wind.
The surface snowpack changes rapidly depending on the aspect and gradient: crusts of various types and thicknesses alternate. In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard crust, especially adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.
The solar radiation will give rise to increasing consolidation of the snowpack.

tendency.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will persist.

regions.name: B - VALLI DI GRESSONEY, AYAS, CHAMPORCHER

avalancheActivity.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will already increase in the late morning. Weak layers in the upper part of the snowpack can be released in some places by people.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The danger of moist and wet avalanches will already be elevated in the early morning. This applies in all aspects at low and intermediate altitudes.
High altitudes: As the day progresses the likelihood of dry and wet avalanches being released will increase. In the event of prolonged bright spells this applies in particular. Avalanches can in particular be released in near-surface layers and reach medium size. Avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on very steep west, north and east facing slopes between approximately 2200 and 2800 m. The avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack can indicate the danger. Avalanches can in isolated cases penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack and reach large size.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be reduced. The surface of the snowpack will already soften in the late morning.
Isolated avalanche prone weak layers exist in the top section of the old snowpack, especially on west, north and northeast facing slopes between approximately 2200 and 2800 m, and on sunny slopes in high Alpine regions.
Towards its base, the snowpack consists of faceted crystals, in particular on west, north and east facing slopes at elevated altitudes.

tendency.highlights: Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be good over a wide area. The avalanche danger will increase a little during the day.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The weather will be very mild. As a consequence of warming during the day and the solar radiation, the likelihood of wet avalanches being released will increase in all aspects above the tree line. Wind slabs require caution. In all aspects slab avalanches are possible.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack can indicate the danger.
The wind slabs can be released even by a single winter sport participant above the tree line, caution is to be exercised in particular at the base of rock walls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain, as well as adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. Additionally in isolated cases avalanches can penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack and reach large size in isolated cases, especially on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2000 m.
The danger of moist and wet avalanches will already increase in the late morning. As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation the prevalence of the avalanche prone locations will increase from the late morning. In particular very steep sunny slopes, intermediate and high altitudes:
In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the icy crust.

snowpackStructure.comment:
These spring-like weather conditions as the day progresses will give rise to moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2500 m.
As a consequence of wind from northerly directions, avalanche prone wind slabs formed in the last few days in particular in gullies and bowls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain, especially in all aspects above approximately 2000 m at elevated altitudes.
The old snowpack will be weakly bonded in some places. In some cases the various wind slabs have bonded still only poorly together.

tendency.highlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow are to be assessed with care and prudence.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Gradual increase in danger of moist and wet avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation small and medium-sized moist and wet avalanches are possible in all altitude zones. More recent wind slabs are clearly recognisable. The wind slabs can be released, especially by large additional loads, in particular on steep shady slopes. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls.
Weak layers exist in the old snowpack on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes. Avalanches can be triggered in the old snowpack reach large size in isolated cases in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

snowpackStructure.comment:
These spring-like weather conditions from the late morning will give rise to moistening of the snowpack in all aspects. The various wind slabs have bonded with each other and the old snowpack.
Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on shady slopes. Avalanches can in some places be released in deep layers in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weakly bonded old snow requires caution. As the day progresses the likelihood of wet avalanches being released will increase.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Dry avalanches can in some cases be released in the old snowpack, this applies even in case of a single winter sport participant in isolated cases. They can penetrate down to the ground and reach medium size. Caution is to be exercised in particular on very steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m, and on south facing slopes in high Alpine regions. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable.
Avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.

As the day progresses the likelihood of wet avalanches being released will increase especially on extremely steep east and west facing slopes. Wet avalanches can occur in particular in starting zones where previous releases have taken place. In some cases these are medium-sized and can be released easily also by a single winter sport participant. Backcountry tours should be concluded timely.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Hard wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m. The snowpack will be subject to considerable local variations.

Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be quite good at times. The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust. In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface. On extremely steep sunny slopes the snowpack will soften rapidly. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to increasing and thorough wetting of the snowpack.

On steep sunny slopes at low and intermediate altitudes hardly any snow is lying.

tendency.highlights: Some starting zones have released the snow, in particular on sunny slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Moist and wet avalanches as the day progresses.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The Avalanche Warning Service currently has only a small amount of information that has been collected in the field, so that the avalanche danger should be investigated especially thoroughly in the relevant locality.

The early morning will see favourable conditions mostly, but the danger of wet avalanches will increase later. As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation the avalanche prone locations will become more prevalent from midday.

The weather will be warm. Backcountry tours and ascents to alpine cabins should be started very early and concluded timely.

The mostly small wind slabs can be released in isolated cases, but mostly only by large additional loads, especially on extremely steep shady slopes at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions. Especially on extremely steep shady slopes the dry avalanches can be triggered in the old snow and reach medium size in isolated cases.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The snowpack is largely stable and its surface has a melt-freeze crust.

The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften earlier than the day before.

As a consequence of a strong wind from northerly directions, hard wind slabs formed since Monday in particular in areas not adjacent to ridgelines. These places are rather rare and are easy to recognise.
In particular very steep shady slopes high altitudes: Individual weak layers exist in the centre of the snowpack.

As a consequence of highly fluctuating temperatures and solar radiation the snowpack will consolidate at the weekend.

tendency.highlights: As a consequence of warming during the day and the solar radiation, the likelihood of dry and wet avalanches being released will increase gradually.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet loose snow avalanches in all aspects.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and will soften during the day. As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation moist and wet avalanches are possible as the day progresses, but they will be mostly small. The more recent wind slabs are easy to recognise and can be released by large loads at their margins in particular.
Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach medium size in some cases.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The small quantity of fresh snow and in particular the wind slabs formed by the strong wind are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack.
The high temperatures from the late morning will give rise to increasing softening of the snowpack in some cases in particular on sunny slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The prevalence of the avalanche prone locations will increase as the day progresses.
avalancheActivity.comment: The wind slabs must be evaluated with care and prudence. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. The wind slabs can still be released. This applies in particular in case of a large load. Restraint should be exercised because avalanches can sweep people along and give rise to falls. As a consequence of warming moist and wet avalanches are possible as the day progresses. The avalanches can be released in deep layers of the snowpack. Gliding avalanches can also occur. Backcountry tours should be started and concluded early.

snowpackStructure.comment: The snowpack's surface remains generally characterized by alternating eroded areas interspersed with deposited zones; in many areas, hard surface crusts are present, locally hidden. Near the crests, evident cornices remain in many places. Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes. The weather conditions as the day progresses will give rise to gradual and thorough wetting of the snowpack.

tendency.highlights:
The weather will be partly cloudy. Continuous warming.
The weather conditions will give rise to gradual and thorough wetting of the snowpack.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and wet snow represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment: In the vicinity of peaks a generally favourable avalanche situation will be encountered in some localities. As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day there will be an increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Above approximately 1500 m small small avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment: As a consequence of warming, the likelihood of wet snow slides and avalanches being released will increase in all aspects in all altitude zones.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet and gliding snow require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment: In all aspects mostly small moist and wet avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on steep sunny slopes.

snowpackStructure.comment: The spring-like weather conditions will give rise to rapid moistening of the snowpack on steep sunny slopes.

avalancheActivity.comment: Avalanches can in very isolated cases penetrate near-ground layers of the snowpack and reach medium size in isolated cases.

snowpackStructure.comment: In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface. The snowpack will be subject to considerable local variations. In particular on steep sunny slopes at low and intermediate altitudes hardly any snow is lying. The weather conditions will give rise to moistening of the snowpack.
